evaluationFactors: Section 1: Response to Project Goals and Scope: 10%
Section 2: Proposed Strategy and Technical Approach: 25%
Section 3: Integration, Scalability, and Future Module Compatibility Narrative: 5%
Section 4: Firm Information, Qualifications, and References: 10%
Attachment A: Software Abilities and Requirements: 30%
overview: The Sites Project Authority (Sites Authority), a California joint exercise of powers authority governing the Sites Reservoir Project, is issuing RFP No. 26-01 to solicit comprehensive proposals for the acquisition and implementation of a cloud-based accounting and financial enterprise resource planning (ERP) software system, including complete support for initial implementation and ongoing services. The Authority currently uses QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024 and seeks a streamlined software package that captures financial transactions systematically, supports budgeting and forecasting, generates reports for informed decision-making, maintains auditable records, and provides dedicated technical support during implementation and throughout the contract term. Success is defined by a fully operational cloud-based ERP system with core modules (Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, Bank Reconciliation, Budget Management, Cash Management, Fixed Assets, General Ledger, Project Accounting, Purchasing, and General & Technical capabilities) going live by January 1, 2027, with the system serving as the Authority's core financial system of record over a long-term horizon that scales to support construction and operations phases. The initial contract term is one year with up to two optional five-year extensions (maximum eleven years total), proposals must not exceed 40 pages, pricing must remain firm for at least three years with subsequent annual increases limited to CPI-U or 3% (whichever is less), and proposals are due April 1, 2026 at 2:00 PM PT via PlanetBids. The Authority anticipates 5-10 ERP users, 10-20 workflow approvers, and 50-100 vendors in 2026, with significant growth expected as the project transitions from planning to final design, construction, and long-term operations of a 1.5 million acre-foot off-stream surface water storage reservoir.

requirements.requirement: Propose an integrated cloud-based ERP solution fulfilling all Core ERP scope items: Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, Bank Reconciliation, Budget Management, Cash Management, Fixed Assets, General Ledger, Project Accounting, Purchasing, and General & Technical

Solution must include software, implementation services, training, project management, and other necessary technology services, either through bidder's own products or in partnership with third-party providers
Import prior one completed fiscal year of transactions, year-end balances, and current fiscal year-to-date transactions from QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024
Provide a Chart of Accounts Mapping showing how ERP codes align with legacy codes
Provide a Configuration Workbook that documents system settings
Provide a Testing and Acceptance Plan
Configure software to meet Sites Authority's requirements and assist with financial report writing
Recommend account structure for auditable reporting and understand auditing and reporting requirements
Provide ongoing cloud hosting services with automated updates and software change communications
Provide help desk support including remote support and on-call assistance, with on-site support available as needed
Solution must be modular, extensible, and capable of supporting project delivery through construction and operations phases
Target go-live date of January 1, 2027; provide proposed high-level timeline and task sequence demonstrating alignment with this objective
Reduce downtime and operational impacts during transition, including implementation-phase support during cutover, go-live, and initial stabilization
Weekly meetings for progress, budget, and timeline monitoring during implementation
Personnel management plan with dedicated project manager and technical staff
Provide at least one recorded training session and electronic training materials for all staff, with role-appropriate and additional training available during implementation and stabilization
Personalized training and manuals for power users
Support for first 12 months post-implementation
Provide documentation demonstrating measures in place for protecting financial data in the SaaS environment and secure access controls implemented (Attachment G)
All data entered into the ERP system shall remain the sole property of the Sites Authority; provide full non-proprietary data exports upon request and upon contract termination at no additional cost
Pricing for software and services actually purchased must remain firm for at least three years from contract execution, with increases limited to annual CPI-U adjustment or 3%, whichever is less
Proposals must be offered for a term not less than 180 calendar days; may not be modified, withdrawn, or canceled during this period
Honor proposed software licensing pricing for optional modules not initially purchased for up to five years from date of proposal submission
Provide proof of insurance including workers compensation and commercial general liability (Attachment F)
Provide certification of financial condition (Attachment E)
Disclose location of workers and identify any use of workers or resources located outside of the United States (Attachment D)
Provide minimum of three references for whom similar services have been performed (Attachment C)
Submit proposed contract agreement for software and services including clauses covering optional scope (Attachment H)
All pricing must be provided in U.S. dollars with clear rationale and basis of calculation for all fees
Pricing shall be on a per-unit basis and shall not assume minimum or maximum purchase quantities unless explicitly requested
Complete and submit Attachment A - Software Abilities and Requirements Excel workbook
Complete and submit Attachment B - Pricing Proposal Excel workbook as a separate file
Identify key personnel anticipated to support implementation and ongoing services
Provide summary of qualifications and relevant experience for key personnel and any proposed subcontractors
Describe availability of solution demonstration resources (e.g., recorded demonstrations, sandbox environments, or trial access)

Provide descriptive and pricing information for optional ERP modules (e.g., capital asset maintenance management, debt management, human resources management, payroll, timekeeping, inventory tracking)
Describe firm's approach to participation by small businesses and businesses owned by minorities, women, or disabled persons, as applicable

format: Electronic proposals in Adobe Acrobat or Microsoft Word format
Proposals shall not exceed 40 pages including transmittal letter and any appendices
Attachment B - Pricing Proposal must be submitted as a separate file
Attachment A - Software Abilities and Requirements must be submitted as a completed Excel workbook
attachmentsAndForms: Attachment A: Software Abilities and Requirements (Excel workbook)
Attachment D: Location of Workers Utilized by Respondent
Attachment E: Certification of Financial Condition

requiredDocuments.name: Transmittal Letter
requiredDocuments.description: Not to exceed two pages, signed by authorized representative, summarizing key points and providing contact information

requiredDocuments.name: Section 1: Response to Project Goals and Scope
requiredDocuments.description: Address approach to meeting proposal goals and producing requested deliverables outlined in Sections 5 and 6, including license types, third-party vendors, and demonstration resources

requiredDocuments.name: Section 2: Proposed Strategy and Technical Approach
requiredDocuments.description: Proposed implementation approach and high-level timeline targeting January 1, 2027 go-live, project governance, support levels, key personnel, training approach, and client engagement model

requiredDocuments.name: Section 3: Integration, Scalability, and Future Module Compatibility Narrative
requiredDocuments.description: Narrative describing how the ERP platform supports expansion to additional modules and integration with third-party systems, including APIs, examples of integrations, and data consistency across modules

requiredDocuments.name: Section 4: Firm Information, Qualifications, and References
requiredDocuments.description: Organization information, firm qualifications and experience implementing accounting/financial software for public agencies, and key personnel experience summaries

requiredDocuments.name: Attachment A: Software Abilities and Requirements
requiredDocuments.description: Completed Excel workbook documenting software abilities and requirements compliance

requiredDocuments.name: Attachment B: Pricing Proposal
requiredDocuments.description: Completed Excel workbook with firm fixed costs for software, implementation, and ongoing services, submitted as a separate file with per-unit pricing

requiredDocuments.name: Attachment C: References
requiredDocuments.description: Minimum of three references for whom similar services have been performed, including client name, scope, performance dates, and valid contact information

requiredDocuments.name: Attachment D: Location of Workers Utilized by Respondent
requiredDocuments.description: Identification of all work locations and disclosure of any use of workers or resources located outside the United States

requiredDocuments.name: Attachment E: Certification of Financial Condition
requiredDocuments.description: Certification regarding current financial condition signed by authorized representative

requiredDocuments.name: Attachment F: Certificate of Insurance
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of insurance including workers compensation and commercial general liability

requiredDocuments.name: Attachment G: Software Application Requirements
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation demonstrating measures for protecting financial data in SaaS environment and secure access controls implemented

requiredDocuments.name: Attachment H: Proposed Contract Agreement
requiredDocuments.description: Respondent's proposed contract agreement for software and services including clauses covering optional scope, non-exclusivity, change-order controls, and pricing validity

requiredVolumes: Transmittal Letter (not to exceed two pages)
Section 1: Response to Project Goals and Scope
Section 2: Proposed Strategy and Technical Approach
Section 3: Integration, Scalability, and Future Module Compatibility Narrative
Section 4: Firm Information, Qualifications, and References (including Sections 4.1, 4.2, 4.3)
Attachment A: Software Abilities and Requirements
Attachment D: Location of Workers Utilized by Respondent
Attachment E: Certification of Financial Condition

deliverables: Approach & Project Management Plan including Chart of Accounts Mapping and Testing and Acceptance Plan
Software Installation & Implementation including data migration from QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024
Staff Training including personalized training and manuals for power users, at least one recorded training session, and electronic training materials
Automated updates and software change communications
Help desk support including remote, on-call, and on-site support as needed
Chart of Accounts Mapping showing ERP codes aligned with legacy codes
